macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Supreme Court Rules Against Trump's Tariff Regime, Sparking Initial Rally Then Pullback in U.S. Futures
items.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled against large parts of President Trump's tariffs, leading to an initial positive response in stocks but premarket futures fell with Dow down 0.35%, S&P 500 down 0.4%, and Nasdaq down 0.5%. Asian markets rose, including Kospi +0.7%, Taiex +2%, and Hang Seng +2.5%, while European indices were mixed.

items.title: SPX Closes at 6909 Up 47 Points Friday, Marking First Up Week Since January
items.summary: The S&P 500 (SPX) rose 47 points to close at 6909 on Friday February 20, up 73 points for the week, with bullish technical indicators including rising moving averages and Bollinger Bands. NASDAQ climbed 203 points to 22,886 with high trading volume.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings After Market Close Wednesday to Provide Key AI Demand Insights
items.summary: Nvidia's fiscal 2026 Q4 earnings, ending January 26, are set for release after Wednesday's close, serving as a bellwether for AI demand given its 1,200% stock rise since early 2023 and 7.4% weight in S&P 500. Investors will focus on guidance and management comments amid ongoing AI hype.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops 2.5% to $65,926 Amid Tariff Uncertainty and Weak ETF Flows
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 2.5% to $65,926 after hitting a two-and-a-half-week low, with Ether down 3.3% to $1,885, as tariff uncertainty reduces risk appetite and Bitcoin ETF flows weaken.

items.title: Major Earnings This Week Including Salesforce, Nvidia, SoundHound AI, and Lucid
items.summary: Key companies reporting earnings this week: Nvidia (Wednesday after close), Salesforce (CRM), SoundHound AI (SOUN), and Lucid Group (LCID), with options volatility highlighting expected moves.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $65,000 Amid Tariff Policy Uncertainty
items.summary: Bitcoin fell as much as 4.8% to $64,300 due to U.S. tariff concerns under Trump, extending a 25% year-to-date decline. Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $3.8 billion in outflows over five weeks, with the crypto market losing $100 billion in the past day.[1]

items.title: Supreme Court Strikes Down Trump Tariffs, Bitcoin Tumbles 5%
items.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court nixed most of Trump's tariffs, causing U.S. futures and Bitcoin to fall, with BTC dipping below $65,000 before partial recovery. Bitcoin has lost nearly half its value since its October high of $126,210 amid regulatory worries.[2]

items.title: Malicious npm Packages Target Crypto Keys in Supply Chain Attack
items.summary: Cybersecurity firm Socket disclosed 19 malicious npm packages in the SANDWORM_MODE campaign stealing crypto keys, CI secrets, API tokens, and targeting Solidity developers via fake VS Code extensions. The malware harvests credentials from LLM providers and developer tools like Claude and VS Code.[3]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moderne Expands OpenRewrite Platform with Python Support
items.summary: Moderne announced support for Python in its OpenRewrite platform, enabling automated refactoring of Python codebases to address vulnerabilities, update dependencies, and implement best practices at scale. This development boosts productivity in data science and web development by automating repetitive tasks.

items.title: University of Kentucky and Microsoft Host 'Cats AI in Action' Showcase
items.summary: The University of Kentucky, in partnership with Microsoft, will host an AI showcase on February 26, 2026, featuring student and faculty demonstrations including accelerated material discovery using machine learning and robot-enabled experiments. The event aims to accelerate AI adoption and foster partnerships across Kentucky.

items.title: Amazon Warns of AI-Assisted Cyber Breaches by Russian Threat Actor
items.summary: Amazon reported that a Russian-speaking threat actor used AI to breach over 600 FortiGate firewalls across 55 countries, highlighting the growing risk of AI-enhanced cyberattacks.

items.title: Cisco and Sharon AI Launch Australia's First Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA
items.summary: Cisco and Sharon AI unveiled Australia's first Cisco Secure AI Factory powered by NVIDIA, delivering secure and high-performance AI infrastructure to support advanced AI deployments.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Latest EDGE Magazine Review Scores for February 2026 Released
items.summary: EDGE magazine's latest issue features reviews for high-profile games including Nioh 3 (8/10), Romeo Is A Dead Man (7/10), 2XKO (7/10), and Highguard (5/10), highlighting strengths and areas for improvement in action, fighting, and shooter genres.

items.title: Pokémon Day 2026 Announcements Previewed with Upcoming Releases
items.summary: Pokémon Day on February 23, 2026, is set to feature announcements including a potential release date for Pokémon Champions, marketing for Pokémon Pokopia launching a week later, and TCG 30th-anniversary expansions; Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen recently launched digitally on Nintendo eShop.

items.title: New Indie Games and Announcements in Weekly Roundup
items.summary: Indie highlights include Fear Effect 2: Retro Helix remake, Reigns: The Witcher crossover, Rise of Piracy, Untamed Kingdom, and others like Ember Bane Metroidvania; Steam Next Festival noted for demos amid fewer new releases.

items.title: Piece by Piece Cozy Sim Sets March PC Launch with New Demo
items.summary: Cozy repair shop sim Piece by Piece, where players help forest animals fix gadgets, launches on PC in March with a new Steam demo featuring updated visuals, UI, and gameplay; Nintendo Switch version pending.

items.title: Zanerdin: The Unbound Turn-Based RPG Announced, No Microtransactions
items.summary: South Korean studio Sanctuary Games unveils debut title Zanerdin: The Unbound, a gacha-free collectible RPG for PC and PS5 focused on strategic battles and story-driven exploration.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem and Nested Lands Top Weekly Releases
items.summary: Key games releasing February 23 - March 1 include Resident Evil Requiem and Nested Lands, spotlighted among the week's top PC and console launches.

items.title: Five Overlooked Steam Releases from Recent Days
items.summary: PC Gamer highlights lesser-known Steam games like Horripilant (Feb 21, puzzle-combat hybrid), Love Eternal (Feb 20, horror platformer), and older drops like F-22: Air Dominance Fighter and Screaming Head.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung and KT Verify 6G Tech in 7 GHz Band
items.summary: Samsung and KT Corporation verified key 6G technology in the 7 GHz band, advancing wireless speeds and IoT connectivity for smart cities. Broadcom also unveiled a new chip supporting both 5G and 6G performance boundaries.[1]

items.title: InterDigital Showcases Wireless Innovations at MWC 2026
items.summary: At Mobile World Congress 2026, InterDigital will demonstrate new services including immersive video experiences and 6G research to expand network capacity and improve user experiences in 5G-Advanced and 6G eras.[2]

items.title: Brunswick Corporation Wins Top Honors for Marine Tech Exhibit at CES 2026
items.summary: Brunswick earned Top 5 Gold in CES 2026 BOSS Awards for its immersive exhibit showcasing marine innovations like intelligence, autonomy, electrification, connectivity, and new products such as Sea Ray SLX 360 and Fliteboard RACE.[5]
